# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

```yaml
version: '3' # Indicate version of the yml file format that this configuration follows – it should always be at top level in a YAML document so clients know about them by default behavior and how to handle unknown fields for backward compatibility. The latest possible specification is available as "v3". (https://yaml.org/spec/)

services: # Start of service definitions block
  webapp:  
    image: your_webapplication_image # Image from where the application will run, should be replaced by real Dockerfile or path to local .jar file if any depending on setup requirements (you may want an official language runtime for that purpose). Use 'python' and then tag it with version. For instance python:3.8-alpine
    ports: # Expose port 5001 in container, mapping this inside the Docker host to a different one if needed(optional) (http://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/services/)     - "${DOCKER_PORT}:5001"
    environment: # Set required ENV variables here for your application, optional – http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .....environm eentures (http://docs.docker.com/engine ...) For security it’s best to use the same Dockerfile and container both runs with different ENV variables
      - SECRET_KEY=1234567890 # Expose an environment variable for your application, optional – http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .. ...environm eentures (http://docs.docker.com/engine ....)
      - SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI=postgresql+psycopg2://user:password@localhost/dbname # Expose an environment variable for your application, optional – http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .. ...environm eentures (http://docs.docker.com/engine ....)
      - REDIS_URL=redis://cache:6379 # Expose an environment variable for your application, optional – http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .. ...environm eentures (http://docs.docker.com/engine ....)
      - DEBUG=True   # You can set the Flask's debug mode here by providing a value of True or False – http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .. ...environm eentures (http://docs.docker.com/engine ....)
    volumes: - "/path_to_yourappdatafolder" # You need to provide a mount point path for your data or volume where Docker can persist the app's state and configuration so it won’t get lost when you delete container(Optional, http://docs......mounting-volumes/)
    depends_on: - redis dbapp  # It will wait until Redis service is up then proceed to next step (http:/... see docker run commands reference https//www . docs /deponage .. ...dependson)
  
  cache:    
      image: "redis"    # Same as web app but we are using redis version, it can be replaced with 'bitnami/redis' if bitnami container available. (http://docs.....)          - depends_on : dbapp              (# This line is commented out and should remain un-commented so the services start in dependency order)
      ports:  # Same as web app, but we will be exposing redis port here for inter service communication         "${DOCKER_PORT}:6379"             (http://docs......ports/)               (# This line is commented out and should remain un-commented so the services start in dependency order)
      restart: unless -stopped  # Restart redis container if it exits, but only when not stated otherwise(Optional). If you want to force Redis always run (you can use "start" or whatever option is provided by docker at https://hub.docker.com/r/bitnami/redis/)
  
  dbapp:     # PostgreSQL service definition block here, replace image name if available in Docker Hub for official postgresql runtime(Optional)            - ports    : "${DOCKER_PORT}:5432"              (http://docs......ports).               (# This line is commented out and should remain un-commented so the services start 
!in dependency order if there's a chance of them not running in their current container environment)                      # Ordering by name, as defined on docker run command. When you stop your service (https://hub .....down). Then rerun with -d to automatically detach and restart the new instances(http:...)
!retain 20m    (# This line is commented out )           & if there's a chance of stopping/starting services, for instance in development when you want container running on port other than specified. (...) # If using this option remember to use your desired ports from the top and replace ${DOCKER_PORT} variable
